name: Kasha-Patate Sucree
recipeInstructions:
This dish has three parts: kasha pilaf, sweet-potato puree and garlic sauce. Begin by preparing the kasha. Thoroughly rinse the groats and discard any excess water. In a medium saucepan, bring the required amount of water to a boil. Add the rinsed groats. Cover and reduce heat to low. Cook the groats for 10 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the groats are fluffy.
Next, prepare the sweet-potato puree. Cut sweet potatoes in halves and bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 minutes, or cut into chunks and boil for 30 - 40 minutes, or microwave chunks for about 7 minutes. Remove skins and discard. If using onions, melt butter in a medium skillet and add the onions. Cook over medium-high heat until the onions are translucent (about 4 minutes). Set aside. In a large mixing bowl, mash the sweet potatoes. Add sauteed onions, milk, salt, and pepper.
For the garlic sauce, blend tamari with vegetable oil and minced garlic in a small bowl. Serve by spooning kasha into a serving dish and drizzling it with garlic sauce. Top with the sweet-potato puree. Serves 6.
